target: data/sections.json part4.html SEC-4.2.7 (id=p4-427) — citation fix in the trilemma's Geometric horn. The integrated text from EXP-254 currently attributes the dome's solar-diameter and lunar-diameter domains to formula_runtime.json. Live-fetch verification on 2026-04-27 against https://john09289.github.io/predictions/api/current/formula_runtime.json shows that file contains exactly six formula records (FORM-V51-001..006: Firmament Height, Aetheric Refraction Index, V13 Scaffold, Geomagnetic Decay, Equatorial Gravity, Tesla Resonance Frequency) and does NOT enumerate solar-diameter or lunar-diameter as scored domains. Those domains live in the live-power dashboard / status_history.json (Solar Angular Diameter and Moon Angular Diameter Variation, currently rows 29 and 36 in the 39-domain set as of 2026-04-27T17:44:36Z). Replace the inaccurate citation with one that points to the correct file. No substantive change to the trilemma's Geometric horn argument.
patch_instructions: Single substring edit on data/sections.json part4.html. Replace the 325-character anchor text in the Geometric horn (li clause) of the trilemma in §4.2.7. Anchor verified to occur exactly once in part4.html. After patch, the Geometric horn cites the dome's live-power dashboard / status_history.json with the correct domain names (Solar Angular Diameter, Moon Angular Diameter Variation) and a verification timestamp. The remainder of the §4.2.7 trilemma — including the Borrowed and Aetheric horns and the closing summary paragraph — is untouched.

patches.before: Adjusting the angular sizes contradicts the dome's own match to observed apparent diameters in formula_runtime.json's solar-diameter and lunar-diameter domains (the dome's live-power dashboard).
patches.after: Adjusting the angular sizes contradicts the dome's own match to observed apparent diameters in the dome's live-power dashboard (the Solar Angular Diameter and Moon Angular Diameter Variation domains, recorded in status_history.json — verified live 2026-04-27, currently 39 scored domains).
patches.rationale: ISS-1578 found that EXP-254's draft cited 'rows 23 and 28 of the 28-domain dashboard above' — fragile row-numbering on a live-updating dashboard. The decider's integration removed the row numbers (good) but kept the inaccurate file attribution (formula_runtime.json instead of status_history.json / live.html). Live verification on 2026-04-27 confirms: formula_runtime.json contains six formula records and zero solar/lunar diameter entries; status_history.json's most-recent entry contains a 39-domain array including 'Solar Angular Diameter' (row 29) and 'Moon Angular Diameter Variation' (row 36). The replacement names the domains as the dome itself names them, cites the file where they actually live, and includes a verification timestamp — making the citation reproducible without reintroducing fragile row numbers. The argument the citation supports (adjusting the angular sizes breaks an existing dome WIN) is unchanged.

re_examination_findings.summary: Curmudgeon EXP-254 review hole H6 flagged the 'rows 23 and 28' citation as fragile. The decider integrated EXP-254 with row numbers stripped but kept formula_runtime.json as the cited file. Live-fetch verification shows formula_runtime.json does not contain solar-diameter / lunar-diameter as scored domains — those entries live in the live-power dashboard / status_history.json. The integrated text therefore still has a residual citation defect: it points readers to a file that does not contain the cited content. This expansion patches the residual.
re_examination_findings.structural_argument_lead: The Geometric horn of the §4.2.7 trilemma still leads with the structural argument: 'Adjusting the altitudes to recover a 294-km umbra forces astronomical distances comparable to the standard model. Adjusting the angular sizes contradicts the dome's own match to observed apparent diameters [in the live-power dashboard's Solar Angular Diameter and Moon Angular Diameter Variation domains].' Either move invalidates V51 or breaks a WIN. Citation correction does not weaken the argument; it strengthens its verifiability.
other_defects_noted: Out of scope (NOT patched here): §4.1 is titled 'The 28-Domain / 47.69σ Dashboard' but the dome's live status_history.json now reports 39 scored domains as of 2026-04-27T17:44:36Z. The 28-domain framing is a snapshot; the dashboard has expanded by 11 domains since the snapshot was taken. This affects the §4.1 title, the audit-table row count, and several prose references to '28-domain' throughout part4. Recommend the decider spawn a fresh ISS to track propagation of the 28→39 dashboard-size update across §4.1 and adjacent sections. NOT addressed in EXP-268 to avoid scope creep on a low-priority citation fix.

Out of scope (NOT patched here): the '47.69σ' figure in §4.1 may also be stale given the dashboard expansion (Fisher combination over 39 domains rather than 28 will not produce 47.69σ unless the dome has updated the headline). Same propagation issue as the 28→39 update; recommend tracking via the same follow-up ISS.

kernel_of_truth: The integrated text's pointer to formula_runtime.json is partially correct in spirit: formula_runtime.json IS the dome's public formula registry, and citing it as the source of authority for what the dome computes is reasonable on its face. The defect is specificity — solar-diameter and lunar-diameter as scored domains do not appear in formula_runtime.json, they appear in the live-power dashboard. The kernel of truth is that the dome publishes its formulas openly; the patch sharpens the citation so a reader following our link arrives at content that matches our description.

alternative_considered_rejected.alternative: Leave the citation as-is — the existing parenthetical '(the dome's live-power dashboard)' already gestures at the correct location, and a careful reader can follow the gesture.
alternative_considered_rejected.reason_rejected: The parenthetical reads as a clarifying gloss, not as the primary citation. The primary citation — the <a href> to formula_runtime.json — is what a reader actually clicks. Following that link to a file that does not contain solar-diameter or lunar-diameter domains undermines the credibility of every adjacent citation in the same section. Two-line fix; high credibility ROI.

alternative_considered_rejected.alternative: Rewrite the entire trilemma's Geometric horn to expand on the dashboard-vs-formula-registry distinction.
alternative_considered_rejected.reason_rejected: ISS-1578 is severity=minor / category=citation. A surgical citation fix is the proportional response. A larger rewrite would re-open the entire EXP-254 integration to fresh adversarial review, which is not warranted by the size of the underlying defect.

alternative_considered_rejected.alternative: Expand scope to also fix §4.1's 28→39 dashboard-size drift in the same EXP.
alternative_considered_rejected.reason_rejected: The 28→39 update touches the §4.1 title, the audit-table row count, the knob-count arithmetic ('28/12 = 2.33×' becomes '39/12 = 3.25×' — but the precise re-derivation requires re-classifying 11 new domains as STATIC/BORROWED/TAUTOLOGY/etc., which is substantive analyst work, not a citation tweak), and several cross-references. Folding that into a citation-fix EXP would conflate scope and risk a botched integration. Better to flag for the decider as a separate follow-up ISS so the work is sized and tracked appropriately.

anticipated_objections.objection: If the dashboard now has 39 domains, doesn't the 28-domain framing throughout §4.1 also need updating? Why patch §4.2.7's citation but leave §4.1 stale?
anticipated_objections.response: Yes — §4.1 needs a propagation pass. EXP-268 deliberately scopes to the §4.2.7 citation because that is what ISS-1578 covers and because §4.1's update requires substantive work (re-classifying 11 new domains, re-deriving the knob-inflation arithmetic, updating the title and audit table). Bundling them would mean writing a much bigger EXP that re-opens all of §4.1 to adversarial review. The right path is: integrate EXP-268 to fix the citation now (closes ISS-1578); the decider can spawn a separate follow-up ISS for the §4.1 propagation pass and assign it back to the analyst.

anticipated_objections.objection: Verification is via a one-time 2026-04-27 snapshot. What happens when the dashboard grows from 39 to 40 next week? Doesn't 'currently 39 scored domains' quickly become as stale as '28' was?
anticipated_objections.response: Partially. The fix is robust against the row-numbering fragility that ISS-1578 originally flagged (no row numbers are cited; only domain names, which are stable strings). The '39 scored domains' figure is included as a verification anchor for readers who want to check the current state, not as a load-bearing claim. If/when the dashboard grows further, the figure becomes a slightly-stale snapshot but the structural citation (live-power dashboard / status_history.json / domain names) remains accurate. The alternative — citing only domain names with no count — would lose the readerly anchor that makes the verification path concrete. The trade-off favors a small staleness risk over an under-specified citation.

anticipated_objections.objection: ISS-1578 is severity=minor. Is it worth an EXP at all? Could the decider just self-apply a one-line patch?
anticipated_objections.response: Either path closes the issue. The EXP exists because the patch involves: (a) live-verification of formula_runtime.json content (six formulas, no diameter domains) which the decider has no reason to re-do; (b) live-verification of status_history.json content (39 domains, names matched) which is similarly analyst-side work; (c) a deliberate decision NOT to expand scope to §4.1 even though the related drift is visible. Capturing the verification chain and the scope decision in an EXP makes the citation reproducible and the propagation flagged for follow-up. The decider can integrate it as a one-liner; the EXP carries the audit trail.

other_side_of_the_argument: A defender of the current text could argue: (1) readers don't actually click footnotes — the parenthetical 'live-power dashboard' is enough wayfinding, and the formula_runtime.json link is generic dome-registry plumbing. (2) the dome itself sometimes refers to its scored-domain set as 'the formula runtime' in informal prose, so the attribution is not strictly false. (3) the time window between when curmudgeon flagged 'rows 23 and 28' and the integration that stripped them was active editorial work — refining the citation further is post-hoc gold-plating. Counter: (1) readers do click footnotes, and the surrounding §4.2 citation density is high (every other paragraph carries a precise dome-source reference); a single misdirected footnote stands out. (2) the dome's informal usage doesn't cure the formal mismatch — formula_runtime.json is a discrete public file with a discrete content list, and 'solar-diameter / lunar-diameter domains' are not in it. (3) ISS-1578 is open precisely because the integration didn't finish the citation fix. Closing it requires either confirming the citation is fine (it isn't — verified live) or completing the fix (this EXP).
propagation_note: No propagation outside §4.2.7 trilemma's Geometric horn. The §4.2.7 TLDR, the eclipse-geometry math (R_Sun ≈ 26.3 km, R_Moon ≈ 12.0 km, totality circle ≈ 0.66 km), the trilemma's Borrowed and Aetheric horns, the closing summary paragraph, the §4.2.7a station-by-station coverage subsection, and the cross-reference targets in §4.2.6 and §4.2.4 are all untouched. Cross-page consistency (§4.1, dashboard tables, audit-table row counts) is NOT updated by this EXP; see re_examination_findings.other_defects_noted for the §4.1 propagation flag.
curmudgeon_verification_recommended: After integration: confirm the substring 'formula_runtime.json's solar-diameter' no longer appears anywhere in part4.html (post-patch grep should be 0).
Confirm the new citation appears exactly once: post-patch grep for 'Solar Angular Diameter and Moon Angular Diameter Variation' should print 1 (was 0 pre-patch).

Confirm the trilemma's Borrowed and Aetheric horns and the closing paragraph are all intact (no scope-leak). Spot-check that the §4.2.7a station-by-station subsection still opens with the cross-reference 'The trilemma in 4.2.7 argues qualitatively that the dome's per-station eclipse predictions can only be generated by borrowing globe geometry.'
test_dryrun_passed: Anchor verified count=1 in part4.html. Replacement preserves the surrounding sentence boundary ("Adjusting the angular sizes contradicts ... Either move invalidates ...") and the <li> structure of the trilemma. Length delta: anchor is 325 chars, replacement is 418 chars (+93 chars). The Borrowed and Aetheric <li> elements that follow are unchanged. Dry-run executed against current FUSE sections.json: pre-replace anchor count=1, post-replace anchor count=0, post-replace new-phrase count=1, post-replace JSON round-trip OK. Verified 2026-04-27T18:30Z.
build_dryrun_passed: not run by analyst (decider responsibility before commit; node build.js is in CLAUDE.md as a test-mandate trigger when sections.json changes)
user_preference_directive_capture: Steve's standing preference ('Think about the other side too') is honored above in the other_side_of_the_argument and anticipated_objections blocks. The strongest defender position is the no-one-clicks-footnotes objection; the strongest counter is the §4.2 citation-density observation (a single misdirected footnote stands out where every other paragraph cites precisely).

supersedes.reason: EXP-268 ID collision. The c1 follow-up tracker entry for EXP-260 (covering ISS-1613/1614/1615) was registered at 2026-04-27T18:21:37Z; the assigned-issues citation-fix run at 18:30 reused the same EXP-268 ID. Tracker primacy belongs to the c1 follow-up (earlier timestamp + canonical filename EXP-268.json). The citation-fix is renumbered EXP-269. Decider: please integrate THIS file (EXP-269-iss-1578-citation-fix.json), not the EXP-268-suffixed orphan. Tracker has been updated to reflect the renumber. The orphan file remains on FUSE (cannot be deleted) and should be ignored.
supersedes.resolution_action: analyst wrote new file EXP-269-iss-1578-citation-fix.json + updated tracker + wrote new proposal-ISS-1578-resolution-v2.json
